metadata.title: MedVault FastAPI Application Component Definition
metadata.remarks: Component Definition for the MedVault FastAPI application and its hardened distroless container. Covers application-layer security controls.

components.title: MedVault FastAPI Application
components.description: Python 3.11 FastAPI application providing the MedVault public-health reporting API, packaged in a hardened distroless container image. Three endpoints: /healthz (CP-2 health check), /readyz (readiness probe), /v1/reports (POST endpoint for jurisdiction-level health reports).
components.purpose: Provide the application-layer functionality of MedVault, packaged for minimum attack surface and structured auditability.

control-implementations.description: Controls satisfied by the FastAPI application component, per the MedVault tailored FedRAMP Moderate profile.

implemented-requirements.description: Runtime least privilege is enforced via the distroless 'nonroot' base image variant, which runs as UID 65532:65532 instead of root. The Dockerfile explicitly sets USER 65532:65532. If a process is compromised, it cannot chown system files, listen on privileged ports, or escalate to root. This complements the IAM-level least privilege implemented by the Terraform component.

Audit record content meets AU-3 requirements via a custom JsonFormatter that emits each log event as a structured JSON document. Each record includes: timestamp (ts), severity level, logger name, message, and a request_id correlation ID propagated via Python ContextVar through the middleware. The request_id is set from the X-Request-Id header (or generated if absent) at request start and reset at request end, and is included on every log line emitted during request handling, enabling SIEM correlation across distributed traces.

Least functionality is enforced through the distroless base image (gcr.io/distroless/python3-debian12:nonroot), which contains only the Python runtime and required shared libraries. No shell, no apt, no curl, no package manager. Combined with a multi-stage build that discards the builder stage, the final image contains nothing beyond what is needed to run the application. This dramatically reduces the attack surface available to a compromised process.

resources.title: MedVault Tailored FedRAMP Moderate Profile
resources.description: The profile this Component Definition references.
